body:
In 3D simulations, we now have the option to upwind both the divergence and the vorticity to allow dissipation of gridscale noise coming from divergent and rotational motions alike.
Upwinding the divergence `δ` is not like upwinding the vorticity `ζ`. 

For the latter, u is transporting `ζ`, while for the former, `u` is the transported quantity since `δ` derives from the advecting velocity (`w`) . 

For this reason, the divergence (`δ`) must be multiplied by the area in z to account for the flux of `w` (as done for the other advecting velocities). 

This small change makes a **_huge_** difference. An example is the solution of a baroclinic adjustment problem on a latlong grid between -60 south and -40 south. The video below shows the surface vorticity as calculated by the main branch (left) and on this PR (right).

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33547697/235322131-8b58fbea-acfb-4b89-ba3d-535e99852436.mp4

## Changes of this PR
- **Breaking change** Rework of the `VectorInvariant` advection scheme. Now the constructor accepts a `vorticity_scheme`, a `vertical_scheme` and an `upwinding` keyword argument which specifies what implementation will be used. The choice is between `OnlySelfUpwinding`, `CrossAndSelfUpwinding` and `VelocityUpwinding` (see documentation)
- Implementation of a 5th order centered WENO scheme to be used for truly multi-dimensional reconstructions
- Implementation of the flux-form advection scheme for `LatitudeLongitudeGrid`s

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: (0.84.0) Improvement of vector invariant advection + various new implementations
